eventMetadata.context_description: Free agent tight end David Njoku's scheduled visit with the Los Angeles Chargers on Monday has propelled them to the top trader consensus at 71.7% implied probability for his 2026-27 landing spot, reflecting strong mutual interest amid LA's push to bolster offensive weapons post-draft. After nine seasons with the Browns, Njoku announced in February he won't return, entering unrestricted free agency following a March post-June 1 release and Cleveland's veteran TE signing plus draft additions at the position. Injury concerns from missing 11 games over the prior two years have kept him unsigned into May, but teams like the Ravens, Dolphins, Cardinals, Broncos, and Panthers—hovering around 45-47%—show persistent demand for his receiving skills, headlined by earlier Baltimore rumors and Miami visit, while Cleveland lingers at 24.5% on slim reunion odds.

markets.question: Total Kills Over/Under 62.5 in Game 2?
markets.description:
In the upcoming match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:30PM ET:

This market is about the total kills in Game 2.

This market will resolve to "Over" if the total kills in Game 2 is 63 or more. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under".

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 begins but is not completed, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 2 is remade, resolution will be based on the total kills in the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.


In Dota 2, a "kill" for markets like team total kills, kill handicap, and interval kills is counted when an enemy hero dies and the game awards a hero kill event to the opposing team's score, which is the number shown on the in-game scoreboard and tracked in scoreboard feeds as team kills. In practical terms, this includes any enemy hero death that increments a team's kill score, regardless of who got the last hit, including situations where the victim dies to creeps, a tower, or neutrals as long as the kill is still credited to the enemy team. It does not include denies, such as killing your own allied units, and it also does not include building or objective destructions like towers, barracks, or Roshan, since those are tracked separately from kills. Assists also do not count as kills, because they are recorded as a separate player stat and multiple players can assist on a single kill.

events.title: Dota 2: Nigma Galaxy vs REKONIX (BO3) - 1win Essence Group B
events.description:
This market refers to the Dota 2 match between Nigma Galaxy and REKONIX in the 1win Essence Group B, initially scheduled for May 2 at 2:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Nigma Galaxy" if Nigma Galaxy win the match against REKONIX.

This market will resolve to "REKONIX" if REKONIX win the match against Nigma Galaxy.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://www.dotabuff.com. However, if https://www.dotabuff.com has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Nigma Galaxy faced REKONIX in a pivotal BO3 group stage matchup at 1win Essence I, a Tier 2 online European tournament running May 2-11 with a $100,000 prize pool and round-robin groups feeding into decider and double-elimination playoffs. As the higher-ranked squad (world No. 13 vs. REKONIX's No. 53), Nigma leveraged their established European pedigree and versatile drafting under patch 7.41 to claim a 2-0 victory on May 2, kickstarting a flawless 3-0 group record (6-0 map differential). REKONIX, a rising Indonesian team with solid recent form (66% win rate), bounced back to 2-2 but highlighted stylistic clashes in aggressive SEA play versus Nigma's macro control. No roster changes or stand-ins noted, with Group B stakes high for top-two advancement.

markets.question: Will JD Gaming qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the LoL Esports website (https://lolesports.com). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/Mid-Season_Invitational/2026) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Qualification slots for MSI 2026, set for late June in Daejeon, South Korea, remain open across major regions, with Road to MSI playoffs culminating in mid-June double-elimination brackets determining the top two teams each from LCK, LPL, LEC, LCS, and LCP, plus CBLOL's champion. LCK frontrunners Hanwha Life Esports (10-1) and KT Rolster (8-2) lead Split 2 standings after dominant early wins, boosting trader consensus on their playoff edge over Gen.G and T1. LEC's Team Vitality (8-1) and Karmine Corp (6-0) mirror this momentum, while LPL sees LGD Gaming and EDG atop group stages. Recent regular season results emphasize form and roster health as key factors amid volatile meta patches.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold a 1-0 lead in their Eastern Conference semifinals series after a dominant 137-98 Game 1 rout of the Philadelphia 76ers on May 4, fueled by Jalen Brunson's 35 points and the Knicks' historic playoff momentum following their first-round sweep of the Hawks. Heading into Game 2 at Madison Square Garden today, the 76ers face a massive setback with Joel Embiid ruled out due to a right ankle sprain and hip soreness, per the latest injury report—downgraded just hours ago after he played through a hip contusion in Game 1. Philly's resilience from rallying past Boston in the first round will be tested without their star center, while New York's home-court advantage, depth, and defensive intensity shape trader consensus amid back-to-back playoff pressure for the Sixers.

Kings Crown sits seventh at 2-3 (4-7 games, 5 points), salvaging momentum with a 2-1 comeback over The World on May 1 but hampered by 0-2 defeats to top contenders HARD GRIND, Team Spirit Academy White, and Aterion. TST boasts perfect win rates on key meta heroes like Khaleed, Harley, and Sora, while KC struggles on picks such as Zhuxin and Kalea; this BO3 clash carries weight for closed qualifier seeding in the B-Tier circuit.

markets.question: Will the Labour Party win at least 300 council seat elections in the 2026 United Kingdom local elections?
markets.description:
United Kingdom local elections are scheduled to be held on May 7, 2026. 

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Labour Party wins at least the listed number of council seats for the United Kingdom's metropolitan boroughs, London borough councils, unitary authorities, county councils, and district councils as a result of this election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of the Labour Party if they are officially nominated by the Labour Party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with the Labour Party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

Only elections for the United Kingdom’s metropolitan boroughs, London borough councils, unitary authorities, county councils, and district councils taking place on May 7, 2026, or rescheduled to within seven days thereafter, will be counted for this market.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant elections, once those results are official. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 United Kingdom local elections aren’t known by April 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 United Kingdom local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the respective council government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ahead of the May 7 local elections across England—including all London borough councils and over 5,000 councillor seats—polling models project Labour suffering half to three-quarters losses from defended positions, driven by national opinion polls showing Reform UK leading at 24% and Labour trailing at 16-17% in late April YouGov surveys. Low government approval, with only one in seven Britons satisfied after nearly two years of Keir Starmer's administration, has fragmented the vote toward Reform and Greens, per MRP analyses highlighting potential record-low Labour performance and no-overall-control outcomes in key councils. Results will clarify the scale, informing trader assessments of Labour's seat totals amid ongoing volatility.

markets.question: Will the Eurozone's 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.6% and 1.8%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

events.title: Eurozone Annual Inflation 2026
events.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the Eurozone over the 12-month period ending December 2026 as reported by Eurostat.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly Eurostat report.

The resolution source for this market will be the Eurostat Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on January 19, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report on the Eurostat website (https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/en/) by selecting the "Database" option from the "Data" dropdown, opening the "Data navigation tree" folder, then the "Economy and finance" folder, then the "Prices (prc)" folder, then the "Harmonised index of consumer prices (HICP) (prc_hicp)" folder, and opening the file named "HICP - monthly data (annual rate of change) (prc_hicp_manr)". The relevant figure can be found in the column for the relevant month in the row marked "Euro area - 20 countries (from 2023)".

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly Eurostat HICP news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/web/main/news/release-calendar or https://www.ecb.europa.eu/press/calendars/statscal/ges/html/sthicp.en.html

eventMetadata.context_description: Eurozone HICP inflation surged to 3.0% year-over-year in April 2026—its highest since September 2023—driven by spiking energy costs from Middle East tensions, lifting trader consensus toward higher annual 2026 averages as reflected in Polymarket's 41.4% implied probability for 3.1%+ and 27.8% for 2.8-3.0%. March's 2.6% print had already exceeded ECB staff projections of 2.6% for full-year 2026 HICP, with the latest Survey of Professional Forecasters revising expectations to 2.7% amid wage persistence and core measures at 2.4%. ECB held deposit facility rate at 2.0% on April 30 but signaled June hike risks via hawks like Nagel and Kazimir, tempering rate-cut bets; full April data due May 20 and May HICP could further shape pricing before year-end resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 25°C on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's 9-day forecast, updated at 16:30 HKT on May 6, projects a maximum temperature of 27°C for May 7 amid mainly cloudy skies with isolated showers and bright periods, east-to-southeast winds at force 3-4, and high probability of significant rainfall, anchoring trader consensus around 27–28°C outcomes. Moderating northeast monsoon influences are delivering cooler maritime air, suppressing diurnal heating despite May's climatological average high of about 29°C, while intermittent sunshine introduces uncertainty that could nudge peaks to 28°C if cloud breaks align with peak solar insolation. Lower odds for 26°C or below reflect minimal cooling risk from showers alone. Traders await the next HKO update at 11:30 HKT on May 7 for refined guidance ahead of resolution based on Observatory station readings.

markets.question: Will "iloveitiloveitiloveit - Bella Kay" be the #1 song on US Spotify this week?
markets.description:
Spotify curates a playlist of the most streamed songs globally and updates it on Fridays to reflect streaming data for the previous week, beginning on the preceding Friday and ending on Thursday.

This market will resolve according to the most-streamed song in the U.S. on Spotify for the week labeled May 8.

If Spotify does not release its top song for the week labeled May 8 by May 9, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will default to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Spotify. The weekly top songs - USA chart can be found on open.spotify.com under the "Charts" heading.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "drop dead," the lead single from her anticipated third album *you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love*, commands a 96% implied probability as the trader consensus frontrunner for US Spotify's #1 weekly chart slot ending May 8, fueled by its explosive recent debut—topping global daily streams at 10.7 million, her career-best, and holding steady at #1 on US daily charts ahead of Ella Langley's surging "Choosin' Texas." This momentum mirrors Rodrigo's prior chart dominators like "drivers license," amplified by viral social buzz, alternate music videos exclusive to Spotify, and preorder-driven fan engagement. With streams tallying over 10 million last week (up from #3), traders see little threat barring an unlikely TikTok explosion for challengers like "Doors" by Noah Kahan or country crossovers; final days before chart lock remain pivotal for any late shifts.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - May 7, 4:45AM-4:50AM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will Renan Santos finish in second place in the first round of the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls such as Real Time Big Data (May 2–4) and Ideia (May 1–5) confirm President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva leading first-round scenarios at 38–40%, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro solidly in second at 33–36%, well ahead of governors Ronaldo Caiado (PSD, 4–6%) and Romeu Zema (Novo, 3–4%). Trader consensus prices reflect this polling average, where Flávio's 65% implied probability for second stems from his surge consolidating bolsonarista support post-April 4 party affiliation window, amid a fragmented opposition field including Renan Santos (Missão) and PT alternatives like Fernando Haddad. Lula's lower 19.5% odds for second underscore expectations of incumbency-driven first-place finish ahead of the October 4 ballot.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket tightly clusters implied probabilities around 12–15°C for Helsinki's highest temperature on May 7, reflecting Finnish Meteorological Institute (FMI) short-range forecasts showing daytime highs near 10–12°C under partly cloudy skies and light southwest winds, aligned with a cool northerly airflow over the Baltic region. This positioning stems from yesterday's model updates, where ECMWF and GFS ensembles exhibit a 3–4°C spread due to uncertainties in cloud cover thickness, solar insolation timing, and high-pressure ridge strength over Scandinavia—factors that could push peaks higher if skies clear earlier. Historical May 7 averages at Helsinki-Vantaa Airport hover at 13°C, tempering downside bets, while FMI's next update tomorrow morning may narrow the gap amid typical early-May variability.

markets.question: Will 25-49 ships transit the Strait of Hormuz between May 4-May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total number of transit calls that IMF Portwatch reports for the Strait of Hormuz for all days from May 4, 2026, through May 10, 2026, inclusive.

Transit calls include container, dry bulk, roll-on/roll-off, general cargo, and tanker ships. Ships not reported by IMF Portwatch will not be considered.

This market will resolve as soon as data has been published for the final date in the specified period. If no data has been published for the final date of the specified period within 14 calendar days (ET) after the end of that period, this market will resolve based on data published up to that point.

Revisions to previously published data points made within this market’s timeframe will be considered. Revisions to previously published data points after data is published for the final date of the specified period, however, will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be IMF Portwatch, specifically the transit calls data published for the Strait of Hormuz at https://portwatch.imf.org/pages/cb5856222a5b4105adc6ee7e880a1730, both in the chart and through downloadable files.

series.title: ships transit the Strait of Hormuz

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors 25-49 ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz during the week of May 4 at 50.5%, reflecting sustained low traffic amid the U.S.-Iran conflict that began February 28, slashing daily commercial vessel passages from a pre-crisis average of 100 to single digits. Recent U.S. Naval Forces Central Command actions, including disabling Iranian-flagged tankers like M/T Hasna on May 6 for ignoring warnings, combined with Iranian territorial claims and safe passage fees, have limited transits to mostly shadow fleet and Iran-linked vessels—33 total last week per satellite data, down from 65 the prior week per shipping trackers. Diplomatic developments, such as President Trump's May 5 pause on U.S. naval escorts for merchant ships and Iran's Revolutionary Guard offer of procedural safe passage, signal potential modest increases, though over 150 non-sanctioned vessels remain stranded in the Persian Gulf, high war risk insurance premiums persist, and mining threats loom, capping odds for 100+ at 11.4%.

markets.question: Spread: Manchester City FC (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Manchester City FC" if Manchester City FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Crystal Palace FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on premierleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Manchester City FC vs. Crystal Palace FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Premier League game, scheduled for March 21 at 11:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester City's home Premier League clash with Crystal Palace, originally set for March 21 at Etihad Stadium, was postponed due to City's participation in the Carabao Cup final against Arsenal the following day, with no rescheduled date confirmed yet—likely in late April or May midweeks. Sitting second in the table with 61 points from 30 matches, nine behind leaders Arsenal, City view this game in hand as pivotal for their title push amid a tight top-four race including Manchester United and Aston Villa. Palace, mid-table battlers, face defensive injury woes with Daniel Munoz, Cheick Doucoure, and Jean Mateta sidelined long-term, while City manage knocks to Nico O'Reilly and Josko Gvardiol alongside Mateo Kovacic's Achilles absence. Recent City form shows rotation depth, but home advantage and superior goal difference favor them upon rescheduling.

markets.question: Will Eluned Morgan be the next First Minister of Wales following the 2026 Welsh parliamentary elections?

This market will resolve to the individual who is formally appointed as First Minister of Wales following the 2026 Welsh parliamentary elections.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as First Minister following the 2026 Welsh parliamentary elections. Any interim or caretaker head of government will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such First Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Wales; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent MRP polls, including Survation's late April projection and More in Common's update, show Plaid Cymru under Rhun ap Iorwerth neck-and-neck with Reform UK for most seats in the May 7 Senedd election, projecting around 33-36 each amid Labour's collapse to 13 seats. Trader consensus at 96% implied probability for ap Iorwerth reflects Plaid's superior coalition prospects in a likely hung parliament, bolstered by prior budget deals with Labour and Reform's limited governing experience or partner appeal. Incumbent Eluned Morgan's admission she may lose her constituency seat underscores Welsh Labour's vulnerabilities. Upsets could arise from Reform surpassing projections to attract allies or an unlikely Labour-Plaid pact retaining Morgan.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service guidance from the Peachtree City office projects a high temperature near 82-83°F at Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport (KATL) today, fueling trader consensus on the 80-81°F (48%) and 82-83°F (41.5%) outcomes amid southwesterly winds and initial partly sunny skies. The close split stems from model uncertainty over afternoon cloud cover and widespread thunderstorms—triggered by high instability and deep moisture—potentially capping peak heating around 2-4 p.m. if precip arrives early, versus allowing brief mid-80s spikes under delayed convective onset. Above the May climatological average of 79°F, real-time KATL observations and 5 p.m. NWS updates will clarify resolution as storm risks escalate into evening.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors self-funded businessman Perry Johnson at 58% implied probability to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, driven by his heavy ad spending and ballot qualification secured in mid-April, positioning him to consolidate undecided voters in a low-turnout contest. Recent polls reflect a tight race: Glengariff Group's late-April survey showed U.S. Rep. John James leading 23%-20% among likely primary voters, while Emerson's mid-April poll had them virtually tied at 21%-20% with 39% undecided. Johnson's lawsuit against James over campaign branding and strong straw poll showings have bolstered his market edge, though James' congressional incumbency and establishment support keep the field competitive ahead of petition validations and potential debates.

markets.question: Will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit (HIGH) $465 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.TSLA%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Tesla, Inc. (TSLA) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Tesla's share price closed at $399.41 on May 6, 2026, up 2.6% intraday amid technical bounces off the multi-year uptrend support near $385, signaling trader consensus on resilience despite Q1 automotive revenue of $17.7 billion missing estimates on softening EV demand. Net income rose modestly to $477 million (13 cents per share), buoyed by AI and software gains, with management hiking 2026 capex 25% to fund robotics, Hardware 5 chip (tape-out complete mid-April), and Robotaxi fleet expansion. eventMetadata.context_description: The South African Weather Service (SAWS) latest forecast pins Cape Town's May 7 maximum temperature at 19°C under partly cloudy skies, aligning with the 31% market-implied probability for that outcome and trader consensus around 17–20°C amid high short-term uncertainty. This positioning stems from the past 48 hours' advisories highlighting an orange-level warning for disruptive rainfall across the Western Cape through May 7, introducing cloudy conditions and cooling influences from a passing low-pressure trough that temper highs below seasonal May averages of 19–20°C. Key variables include cloud cover variability across forecast models like ECMWF and GFS ensembles, potential berg wind warming if high pressure builds post-front, and frontal passage timing, all contributing to the tight probability spread. New SAWS updates and model runs expected daily could refine odds as resolution nears.

markets.question: Will NYC Mayor post 180-199 posts from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani (@NYCMayor), posts on X between May 1, 12:00 PM ET and May 8, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

markets.question: Will François Asselineau be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: One year before France's 2027 presidential election first round (April 11 or 18), Jean-Luc Mélenchon's May 4 declaration as La France Insoumise candidate highlights a crowded field with over a dozen official entrants, including Les Républicains' Bruno Retailleau (April 19) and Horizons' Édouard Philippe. Rassemblement National traders favor Jordan Bardella amid Marine Le Pen's pending July 7 appeals ruling on her five-year ineligibility from embezzlement conviction. Left-wing primary on October 11 and September senatorial elections may consolidate nominations, but all face securing 500 parrainages from elected officials across 30 departments by early March 2027. March municipal elections curbed far-right gains in cities, underscoring urban ballot challenges despite Bardella's poll leads.

markets.question: Will Gardner Minshew start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Justin Fields sits at 25.5% following his March trade from the Jets as primary insurance, with Andy Reid's April endorsement calling him a "legitimate starting NFL QB" ready for early reps if needed. Chalamet's odds dipped to 14.5% amid awards-season setbacks and minor backlash, while breakout "Heated Rivalry" heartthrobs Connor Storrie (9.5%) and Hudson Williams (9.0%) sustain buzz from viral red carpets, GQ shoots, and HBO Max streaming traction since January's Golden Globes debut. Ryan Gosling holds steady at 11.3% on enduring appeal, but the fragmented field underscores volatility—summer projects, social media virality, or surprise endorsements could swing the closely contested race before November's reveal.

markets.question: Will Akamai Technologies (AKAM) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Akamai Technologies is estimated to release earnings on May 7, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Akamai Technologies’s non-GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $1.60 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Akamai Technologies reports non-GAAP EPS greater than $1.60 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the non-GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Akamai Technologies releases earnings without non-GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the non-GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents; or, if not published there, according to the GAAP EPS provided by SeekingAlpha. If no GAAP EPS number is available from either source at that time, the market will resolve to “No.” (For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless it is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.)

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced non-GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for non-GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: If multiple versions of non-GAAP EPS are published, the market will resolve according to the primary headline non-GAAP EPS number, which is typically presented on a diluted basis. If diluted is not published, then basic non-GAAP EPS will qualify.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Slovenia on March 22, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially elected by the National Assembly (Državni zbor) and sworn in as Prime Minister of Slovenia following the next parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally sworn in. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is sworn in by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Slovenia; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 2026 Slovenian parliamentary elections, where no party secured a majority in the 90-seat National Assembly, outgoing Prime Minister Robert Golob's Freedom Movement failed to form a coalition and conceded on April 20, shifting momentum to Janez Janša's Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) and its right-wing allies including New Slovenia (NSi) and Resni.ca. Recent parliamentary passage of a Government Act reducing ministries from 19 to 14 with 49 votes demonstrated bloc cohesion, while Janša announced on May 5 that coalition guidelines are agreed, with final details expected within a week ahead of a confidence vote. Trader consensus at 93% reflects this advanced stage in coalition negotiations under Slovenia's proportional representation system, though a breakdown over policy sticking points or presidential intervention could trigger fresh talks or snap elections.

markets.question: Spread: RC Strasbourg Alsace (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for May 10 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "RC Strasbourg Alsace" if RC Strasbourg Alsace win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Angers SCO".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: RC Strasbourg Alsace hold an advantageous position in the Ligue 1 table around 8th with 46 points from 30 matches, fueled by a dramatic 3-2 injury-time comeback victory at Lorient on April 26 that highlights their resilience amid a mixed run including losses to Rennes and Nice. Angers SCO, mired in 13th on 34 points from 31 games, struggle with poor recent form—drawing 1-1 versus Le Havre, losing 0-3 to PSG, and conceding heavily to Rennes and Lens—exacerbated by key injuries to Carlens Arcus (groin), Djibirin Harouna (ankle), and Yassin Belkhdim (arm). Strasbourg dominated earlier this season 5-0 at home, though Angers benefit from Stade Raymond Kopa home advantage and potential rest before matchday 33 on May 10. Trader consensus reflects Strasbourg's superior standing, momentum, and squad depth despite their own absences like Emanuel Emegha (muscle tear).

markets.question: Will the Republicans win the 2028 US Presidential Election?
markets.description:
The 2028 United States presidential election will be held on Tuesday, November 7, 2028.

This market will resolve to the political party whose candidate is elected the next President of the United States in the 2028 election.

The resolution source for this market is the Associated Press, Fox News, and NBC. This market will resolve once all three sources call the race for the same party. If all three sources haven’t called the race for the same party by the inauguration date (January 20, 2029) this market will resolve based on who is inaugurated.

President Trump's 2024 victory and constitutional term limits bar his reelection, shifting dynamics to favor the out-party historically in non-incumbent races, with the electoral map mirroring 2024's battlegrounds like Pennsylvania, Wisconsin, and Michigan. Recent Echelon Insights polling from late April shows shifting preferences among potential nominees—JD Vance leading Republicans, a fragmented Democratic field boosting party odds—while 2026 midterms loom as a pivotal test of GOP midterm vulnerabilities and turnout in swing states.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on the constructor team that achieves pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix, scheduled for May 23, 2026.

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

events.title: Elon Musk # tweets May 8 - May 15, 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 8 12:00 PM ET to May 15, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Elon Musk tweet count market for May 8-15 tightly clusters around 140-199 posts, with 160-179 (19.5%) narrowly leading 180-199 (17.5%) and 140-159 (16.0%), reflecting uncertainty over his post-trial posting rebound. Musk's recent X activity dipped to an average of ~10-17 daily tweets during the ongoing OpenAI lawsuit testimony—peaking at 68 on May 1 but plummeting to single digits by May 3-6 amid court demands—yet historical patterns show 20-25 per day during active periods like April 24-May 1's 180-199 total. Key swing factors include trial conclusion timing, potential viral threads on SpaceX/xAI deals or political memes, and X platform engagement spikes, with resolution hinging on sustained 18-25 daily output free from legal distractions.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Los Angeles be between 54-55°F on May 7?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Los Angeles International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 7 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Los Angeles International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/los-angeles/KLAX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket clusters around 64-72°F for Los Angeles' highest temperature at LAX on May 7, with a slight edge to 72°F or higher at 26.5%, reflecting uncertainty in marine layer clearing amid cool sea surface temperatures near 59°F off the Southern California coast. Recent National Weather Service guidance for May 4-5 shows observed highs of 66-68°F under persistent stratus clouds and light onshore flow, tempering diurnal heating despite a developing weak upper-level ridge. Model ensembles like GFS and ECMWF diverge by 4-6°F, with potential for later clearing boosting upper outcomes; new 00Z runs expected tonight could sharpen probabilities ahead of resolution. Historical May averages hover near 70°F, underscoring the tight balance of coastal cooling versus inland warming influences.

markets.question: Spread: Club Independiente Petrolero (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Copa Sudamericana game, scheduled for April 15 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Club Independiente Petrolero" if Club Independiente Petrolero win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Caracas FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on conmebol.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Caracas FC vs. Club Independiente Petrolero - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Copa Sudamericana game, scheduled for April 15 at 6:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Caracas FC hosts Club Independiente Petrolero in the Copa Sudamericana group stage opener on April 15 at Estadio Olímpico de la UCV, where home advantage could prove key amid Caracas's struggles in Liga FUTVE Apertura—sitting 11th with a 1-4-3 record, including recent draws like 1-1 versus Monagas and losses exposing defensive frailties. Independiente Petrolero, mid-table in Bolivia's Primera División with mixed results such as a recent 0-0 stalemate against Guabirá, brings solid away form but faces travel and adaptation challenges. No major injuries or suspensions reported for either side, and limited head-to-head history leaves the matchup closely contested based on trader consensus, with group positioning implications heightening stakes.

markets.question: Will Kylian Mbappe be the 2025/2026 top UCL goal scorer?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the top goalscorer of the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League.

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the Green Party win the most council seat elections in the 2026 United Kingdom local elections?
markets.description:
United Kingdom local elections are scheduled to be held on May 7, 2026. 

This market will resolve according to the party whose official candidates win the most council seats for the United Kingdom's metropolitan boroughs, London borough councils, unitary authorities, county councils, and district councils in the 2026 United Kingdom Local Elections.

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of a party if they are officially nominated by that party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with that party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

Only elections for the United Kingdom’s metropolitan boroughs, London borough councils, unitary authorities, county councils, and district councils taking place on May 7, 2026, or rescheduled to within seven days thereafter, will be counted for this market. A party will have won as soon as it becomes mathematically impossible for any other party to equal or surpass its number of wins in these elections.

In the case of a tie between two or more parties for the greatest number of relevant council seat elections won, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose name comes first in alphabetical order, as listed in this market group.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant elections, once those results are official. This market will remain open until a party has won or until the results of all of the relevant elections are made official. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 United Kingdom local elections aren’t known by April 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, the winning party will be determined based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 United Kingdom local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the respective council government.

While probabilities exceed 90%, realistic challenges include tactical voting, differential turnout favoring incumbents, candidate vetting controversies, or last-minute scandals altering momentum in key battleground councils.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors a SpaceX IPO closing market cap exceeding $2 trillion at 52.5% implied probability, driven by the company's April 2026 confidential S-1 filing that escalated its target valuation above $2 trillion from an initial $1.75 trillion, following a December 2025 tender offer at $800 billion amid surging Starlink satellite constellation revenue—nearing $16 billion in 2025—and reusable Falcon 9 launch dominance. Starship development milestones, including over $15 billion invested in the fully reusable super-heavy booster for interplanetary missions, bolster growth projections, positioning adjacent outcomes like $1.8T–2.0T (13%) as runners-up. With a June 2026 roadshow looming, traders eye regulatory nods and market demand, though execution risks like Starship certification delays could pressure lower strikes.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Dina Titus's commanding fundraising lead—$1.5 million raised and over $1 million cash-on-hand—over Democratic primary challengers Joy Hoover, Gabriel Cornejo, and Luis Paniagua bolsters trader consensus for a Democratic hold at 84% in Nevada's 1st Congressional District, a D+2 partisan lean area. markets.question: Will Franco Colapinto be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

Traders price Antonelli at 39.4% implied probability as the consensus favorite, reflecting his blistering form in the superior Mercedes machinery, while Russell's consistent podiums position him second at 26.5%. Lando Norris (11.1%) trails further back on 51 points with McLaren showing occasional competitiveness, but Red Bull's struggles have dropped Max Verstappen to 5.9% odds, underscoring Mercedes' early-season stranglehold with 11 races remaining.

markets.question: Will there be no head of state of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Nicolás Maduro (62%) as Venezuela's head of state by December 31, 2026, despite his January 3, 2026, capture by U.S. forces during a military intervention, reflecting skepticism over a full political transition and potential continuity in international recognition, such as by the United Nations. Delcy Rodríguez (23.5%), sworn in as acting president by the Supreme Court and recently granted U.S. sanctions relief, leads alternatives amid resumed commercial flights from Miami on May 1 and returning U.S. energy firms like Hunt Oil, signaling pragmatic normalization over four months post-capture. Opposition leader María Corina Machado (8.5%) trails due to her exile status and sidelined role, with no free elections scheduled amid persistent political prisoners and institutional control by chavismo allies like Jorge Rodríguez. Upcoming transition talks, urged by U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, could shift dynamics.

markets.question: Will Bitcoin dip to $50,000 by December 31, 2026?

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Low" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Aħwa Maltin win the most seats in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election?

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the House of Representatives (parliament of Malta) as a result of the 2026 Maltese general election.

If the results of this election are not known definitively by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

Parties will primarily be ranked by the number of seats won. In the event of a tie, the party with the greater number of valid votes will rank higher. If a tie persists, the party whose name appears first in alphabetical order will rank higher. This market will resolve to the party that occupies the highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the House of Representatives in the 2026 Maltese general election, not any coalition it may be a part of.

This market will resolve based on the results of the 2026 Maltese general election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Tokyo be 19°C on May 8?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 8 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment clusters tightly around 24–27°C outcomes, reflecting the Japan Meteorological Agency's latest forecast issued May 6, which projects a Tokyo high of 26°C on May 8 amid cloudy conditions and stable atmospheric patterns over the Kanto region. This follows recent observational data showing a May 5 high of 22°C, with a warming trend as post-Golden Week high-pressure influences strengthen, aligning with May climatological averages of 22–25°C but allowing for variability. Model ensembles indicate minor spread due to cloud cover potentially capping temperatures at 24–25°C or brief clearing pushing toward 27°C+, compounded by urban heat island effects and sea breeze moderation. Traders await JMA's next update on May 7 for refined guidance, underscoring inherent short-range forecast uncertainty.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices West Ham United FC, Draw, and Arsenal FC at 49.5¢ each for the halftime result, underscoring a fiercely competitive London derby where first-half caution prevails amid late-season stakes. West Ham, battling mid-table security with a gritty 2-1 home win over Everton on April 25 and a resilient 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace days earlier, leverage London Stadium's fortress-like atmosphere and solid home form against goal-shy opening periods. Title frontrunners Arsenal, who reclaimed Premier League summit via a narrow 1-0 victory versus Newcastle on the same weekend, face injury headwinds including Bukayo Saka's ankle issue, Martin Ødegaard's knee problem, and doubts over Declan Rice and Jurrien Timber, tempering their attacking fluency early. Head-to-head history shows tight first halves, amplifying the even-market dynamics.

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.
